sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Kelly Hotel & Lodging Sector ETF (HOTL) stock price, news, quote and history
feed.summary: This page provides detailed financial information for the Kelly Hotel & Lodging Sector ETF (HOTL), including its current stock price, historical data, performance overview, and key metrics. It highlights the ETF's investment strategy, focusing on companies in the hotel and lodging business, and lists its sector weightings and related tickers. The fund aims to track the total return performance of an index of hotel and lodging companies using a passive management approach.

feed.title: Total non-current liabilities of Gates Industrial Corporation plc – NYSE:GTES
feed.summary: This page provides financial data for Gates Industrial Corporation plc (NYSE:GTES), specifically focusing on its total non-current liabilities. It also lists various financial tools and resources available through TradingView. The article serves as a direct access point to GTES's financial performance metrics.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Begins Coverage on Pinterest (NYSE:PINS)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has initiated coverage on Pinterest (NYSE:PINS) with an "overweight" rating and a $25.00 price target, suggesting a potential upside of 36.8% from its previous close. This new rating comes as Pinterest is trading near $18.27 and recently reported a slight miss on EPS and revenue, though it still showed 14.3% year-over-year revenue growth. The overall analyst consensus for Pinterest remains a "Hold" with an average target price of $25.42.

feed.title: Arm Holdings slides as Morgan Stanley downgrades (ARM:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Arm Holdings shares experienced a 3.7% drop in premarket trading following a downgrade from Morgan Stanley. The firm lowered its rating on Arm to Equal-Weight from Overweight, citing Arm's transition into chip making as a structural evolution of its business model. This change in business model is seen as a key factor in the re-evaluation of the company's stock outlook.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) PT Lowered to $55 at Evercore ISI
feed.summary: Evercore ISI analyst Oliver Wintermantel has lowered the price target for Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) to $55. This detail is part of a premium article, requiring a StreetInsider.com Premium subscription to access the full content.

feed.title: Oceania Cruises® Announces the Reinspiration of Oceania Marina™
feed.summary: Oceania Cruises has announced a comprehensive reinspiration of its ship, Oceania Marina, scheduled for dry dock in October 2026. This stem-to-stern transformation is part of the Oceania NEXT program, aiming to redesign staterooms, enhance public spaces, and align the ship's aesthetic with the brand's modern luxury vision and upcoming newbuilds. Key highlights include redesigned staterooms with new bathrooms, refreshed suites and public areas, the introduction of a Founders Bar, a new Chef's Studio, the addition of The Bakery to Baristas, and enhanced specialty restaurants.

feed.title: Bank of America Named No. 1 Nonprofit OCIO Provider Globally in 2025
feed.summary: Bank of America has been recognized as the leading nonprofit outsourced chief investment officer (OCIO) provider globally for 2025. This prestigious ranking highlights the bank's strong commitment to serving the financial needs of nonprofit organizations worldwide.

feed.title: Alloy Therapeutics Enters into Multi-Target Collaboration and License Agreement with Biogen for Use
feed.summary: Alloy Therapeutics has announced a multi-target collaboration and license agreement with Biogen for the use of Alloy’s AntiClastic™ ASO Platform. This partnership aims to accelerate the development of innovative oligonucleotide therapeutics, building on a previous collaboration between the two companies. Alloy will receive an upfront payment and is eligible for additional milestone payments and royalties on any resulting products.

feed.title: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) grants 4,623 RSUs to Chief Actuary Strommer
feed.summary: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) Chief Actuary Richard Paul Strommer was granted 4,623 ordinary-share-linked restricted stock units (RSUs) as equity compensation, increasing his direct holdings to 72,648 ordinary shares. These RSUs, granted at no cash cost, will vest in equal installments over three years on January 1, contingent on his continued service. This transaction is categorized as routine stock-based compensation rather than an open-market purchase or sale.

feed.title: PNC's new bank rewards plan gives bigger perks at $25,000 and up
feed.summary: PNC Bank has launched PNC TotalRewards, a new tiered loyalty program that offers enhanced benefits to customers based on their 90-day average eligible balances. Clients can qualify for Silver, Gold, or Platinum tiers with balances starting at $25,000, $100,000, and $500,000 respectively, receiving perks like improved card rewards, elevated savings rates, and priority servicing. The program aims to deepen client relationships and provide greater value, with an automatic Silver Tier qualification for military members.

feed.title: Director at Howmet (NYSE: HWM) receives 155-share stock award
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace director Joseph S. Cantie was granted 155 shares of common stock on April 2, 2026, at a price of $232.68 per share. This stock award increased his direct holdings to 43,217 shares and is considered a routine compensation-related equity grant rather than an open-market transaction. The Form 4 filing indicates a neutral impact and sentiment regarding the transaction.

feed.title: CEO of Greenlight Capital Re (NASDAQ: GLRE) gets 26,417 RSUs
feed.summary: Greenlight Capital Re (NASDAQ: GLRE) CEO, Greg Richardson, received an equity grant of 26,417 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of his compensation. These RSUs will vest evenly over three years on January 1st, contingent on his continued service. Following this grant, Mr. Richardson directly holds 106,508 ordinary shares in the company.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of PayPal Holdings, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of PayPal Holdings, Inc. investors who purchased securities between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal created a false impression about its projected revenue outlook and growth, while minimizing risks, and that its optimistic growth plans were not achievable. This action follows a significant drop in PayPal's stock price after the company announced disappointing Q4 2025 results, withdrew its 2027 financial targets, and attributed issues to macroeconomic factors and operational problems.

feed.title: High costs squeeze new U.S. retail projects as construction pulls back
feed.summary: U.S. retail construction activity significantly declined in Q1 2026, reaching near multi-decade lows with only 64.2 million square feet underway. This pullback is attributed to soaring land prices, construction costs, and interest rates, making new developments financially challenging despite strong demand in some markets. Developers are also cautious due to past supply risks and competition for sites from other property types.

feed.title: Factories and utilities push AI into operations; networks decide scale
feed.summary: Cisco's latest "State of Industrial AI Report" reveals that 61% of industrial organizations are actively using AI in live operations, with 20% reporting mature deployments. The report emphasizes that network readiness, robust cybersecurity, and strong IT/OT collaboration are critical factors determining the successful and safe scaling of AI in physical systems, factories, utilities, and transportation. Most organizations plan to increase AI spending, but deployment challenges arise from readiness gaps in infrastructure, security, and skills.

feed.title: DXC Partners with ServiceNow on a New Wave of AI-first Enterprise Transformation
feed.summary: DXC Technology and ServiceNow have announced a new multi-year partnership to modernize enterprise operations by deploying AI at scale. DXC will act as "Customer Zero" for ServiceNow's Core Business Suite, integrating agentic AI into its Global Business Services model to optimize its own operations. This internal deployment will create validated AI use cases that DXC will then offer to its global customers, accelerating their AI-driven transformations.

feed.title: Oceania's 1,250-guest Marina gets new rooms, bakery, Chef's Studio
feed.summary: Oceania Cruises announced a comprehensive "reinspiration" of its 1,250-guest ship, Oceania Marina, scheduled for dry dock in October 2026. This overhaul, part of the OceaniaNEXT program, includes redesigned staterooms and bathrooms, refreshed suites and public spaces, and the addition of a new Chef's Studio, Bakery at Baristas, and Founders Bar. The initiative aims to align the fleet's aesthetics with future newbuilds while maintaining the brand's high culinary and service standards.

feed.title: Globalstar rolls out private 5G system for AI-driven industry
feed.summary: XCOM RAN by Globalstar has launched an end-to-end private 5G solution to accelerate the adoption of physical AI in industrial automation. This new system, based on O-RAN standards, offers spectrum flexibility, including dedicated use of Globalstar Band n53, and features a multi-tenant management system to simplify deployment. The solution is designed to support high-capacity requirements for AI-driven industrial environments and integrates with a network of industry partners for seamless deployment.

feed.title: Terns holders get $53 a share as Merck opens takeover offer
feed.summary: Merck has commenced a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Terns Pharmaceuticals for $53.00 net in cash per share. The acquisition, initially announced on March 25, 2026, will make Terns a wholly owned subsidiary of Merck upon successful completion. The tender offer is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, provided certain conditions, including the tendering of more than 50% of Terns' shares and regulatory approvals, are met.

feed.title: First responders gain satellite radio links in dead zones under new deal
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions and T-Mobile have partnered to integrate satellite connectivity into Motorola's APX NEXT smart radios, providing uninterrupted communication for first responders in remote areas. This collaboration extends the reach of mission-critical AI and communication tools, leveraging T-Mobile’s T-Satellite and 5G technology, ensuring reliable voice and data services even where traditional cellular towers are unavailable. The enhanced APX NEXT radios are FedRAMP High authorized, meeting stringent government security standards for public safety operations.

feed.title: Xoople and L3Harris Announce the Co-Development of an Unprecedented Space-Borne Measurement System Designed for the AI Era
feed.summary: Xoople and L3Harris Technologies have announced the co-development of a first-of-its-kind satellite constellation optimized for the AI era. This constellation, resulting from seven years of R&D, aims to provide real-world context for AI systems by delivering highly precise and rapid spatial intelligence. Xoople refers to this infrastructure as "Earth’s System of Record," designed to support various applications from supply chain optimization to geopolitical monitoring.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Enphase Energy, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Enphase Energy made false or misleading statements regarding its channel inventory management, mitigation of effects from the termination of the Residential Clean Energy Credit, and its overall financial prospects, leading to a stock price drop of over 15% after the company disclosed negative financial outcomes. Investors have until April 20, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Allergan Aesthetics Third State-of-the-Art AMI Center Opens in Austin
feed.summary: Allergan Aesthetics has opened its third U.S. Allergan Medical Institute (AMI) training center in Austin, Texas, on April 7, 2026. This state-of-the-art facility will provide healthcare professionals with hands-on education in facial injectables, body contouring, skincare, and regenerative medicine, reinforcing Allergan's commitment to advancing aesthetic education. The center, directed by Dr. Saami Khalifian, aims to enhance clinical proficiency, complication management, and practice development for providers.

feed.title: NFL taps U.S. Bank for player finance program, Super Bowl MVP
feed.summary: U.S. Bank has announced a multi-year partnership with the NFL, becoming its official bank and wealth management sponsor. This collaboration includes U.S. Bank sponsoring the Super Bowl MVP Award and the NFL FLAG Championships, as well as launching the U.S. Bank Financial Edge™ program to provide financial education to NFL players. The initiative aims to support players throughout their careers and beyond, building on a two-decade banking relationship between the two organizations.

feed.title: U.S. Bank becomes official bank of NFL in multi-year sponsorship deal
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp has secured a multi-year agreement to become the official bank and wealth management sponsor of the NFL. This deal significantly strengthens U.S. Bank's existing ties with the country's largest sports league.

feed.title: Ace Hardware Joins Uber Eats to Expand Local, On-Demand Delivery for Customers Nationwide
feed.summary: Ace Hardware has partnered with Uber Eats to offer on-demand delivery from over 3,700 of its stores across the U.S. This collaboration allows customers to quickly order home improvement essentials through the Uber Eats app, leveraging Ace's local presence and Uber's delivery technology. This move expands Uber Eats' offerings beyond food into general retail, providing convenience for a variety of consumer needs.

feed.title: Peru’s Army buys aircraft for medical evacuations and disaster aid
feed.summary: The Peruvian Army has acquired a Beechcraft King Air 360C aircraft from Textron Aviation to enhance its capabilities in medical evacuations, patient transport, and disaster relief. This twin-engine turboprop, expected to be in service by 2028, will deliver critical services to remote regions across Peru. This purchase strengthens Textron Aviation's relationship with the Peruvian Army, including an offset program providing advanced training and support.

feed.title: Textron Aviation Receives First Military Order for Cessna SkyCourier, Launching the Aircraft Into Global Defense Market
feed.summary: Textron Aviation announced that Belgium has ordered five multirole Cessna SkyCourier aircraft for its Special Operations Forces, marking the model's entry into the global defense market. Deliveries to the prime contractor Sabena Engineering are expected by 2027, followed by in-country military modifications. This selection highlights the SkyCourier's robust performance, low operating costs, and adaptability for critical defense missions, while also boosting local industrial expertise through Sabena's modification work.

feed.title: Cessna SkyCourier Strengthens Mission Versatility With New In‑Flight Operable Door Option
feed.summary: Textron Aviation has announced a new In-Flight Operable Door option for the passenger variant of its Cessna SkyCourier turboprop, enhancing its versatility for special missions in military, humanitarian, and commercial operations. This specialized modification, expected by 2028, will allow in-flight deployment of personnel or cargo, expanding opportunities for various operators. The SkyCourier is recognized for its reliability and adaptability, now further tailored for rapid deployment and operational flexibility.

feed.title: Targeting software for guided weapons now runs the whole mission
feed.summary: Teledyne FLIR OEM has upgraded its Prism SKR software, transforming it from an automated targeting system into a unified, closed-loop autonomy platform for guided weapon systems. This enhancement integrates mission execution and intelligent supervision, setting a new industry standard for defense applications including loitering munitions and counter-UAS platforms. The updated software offers advanced features like 3D interactive aimpoint selection and pixel-lock targeting, operating efficiently on low-power embedded platforms.

feed.title: Synchrony's CareCredit Credit Card Now Accepted on Walmart.com
feed.summary: Synchrony announced that its CareCredit credit card is now accepted for eligible health and wellness product purchases on Walmart.com, in addition to Walmart and Sam's Club stores nationwide. This expansion allows cardholders to use CareCredit for new product categories, including medical supplies, fitness equipment, and sleep essentials, both online and in-store. The collaboration aims to enhance access and convenience for consumers seeking health and wellness solutions, leveraging CareCredit's extensive network and Walmart's retail presence.

feed.title: Stanley Black & Decker Recognized as one of America's Most Innovative Companies
feed.summary: Stanley Black & Decker has been recognized as one of America's Most Innovative Companies by Fortune, securing the No. 36 ranking. This achievement reflects the company's commitment to innovation across its products, processes, and culture, with a significant rise of nearly 60 spots from the previous year. Key innovations include advancements in its DEWALT brand, such as a partnership with August Robotics for data center construction and additions to the DEWALT POWERSHIFT™ line.

feed.title: DuPont launches ultrafiltration modules with integrated pre-filter
feed.summary: DuPont has introduced Inge™ ultrafiltration modules with integrated pre-filter (iP-F), combining pre-filtration and ultrafiltration into a single unit. This innovation aims to reduce plant footprint and lower capital and operational costs for drinking water and seawater applications. The new modules offer consistent permeate quality and pathogen removal in a more compact design, suitable for new installations, expansions, and containerized systems.

feed.title: New DuPont filter packs two water-treatment steps into one unit
feed.summary: DuPont (NYSE: DD) has launched Inge ultrafiltration modules with an integrated pre-filter (iP-F), combining pre-filtration and ultrafiltration into a single unit. This innovation is designed to reduce plant footprint, lower capital and operational costs, and simplify plant design for drinking water and seawater applications. The new modules maintain existing membrane materials and drinking water certifications, while offering consistent permeate quality and pathogen removal.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson brings next-gen Varipulse PFA catheter to Europe
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson has launched its Varipulse Pro system in Europe following CE mark approval. This next-generation pulsed field ablation (PFA) catheter features a new pulse sequence designed to improve procedural efficiency and reinforce safety and effectiveness. The technology offers a lower temperature profile and faster ablation while maintaining integration with the Carto 3 mapping system for precise lesion delivery.

feed.title: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) awards 6,395 RSUs vesting over three years
feed.summary: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) has awarded 6,395 restricted stock units (RSUs) to its Head of Innovations, Brian Joseph O'Reilly. This compensation grant, valued at $0.0000 per share, will vest evenly over three years on January 1st, contingent upon O'Reilly's continuous service with the company. Following this grant, he directly holds 103,530 ordinary shares.

feed.title: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) awards CFO 7,705-share equity grant
feed.summary: Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) has granted its Chief Financial Officer, Faramarz Romer, 7,705 ordinary shares as equity compensation. These shares are part of a Restricted Stock Unit award that will vest evenly over three years, contingent on his continuous service with the company. Following this grant, the CFO directly holds 160,186 ordinary shares in Greenlight Capital Re.

feed.title: Controller at Greenlight Capital Re (GLRE) receives 3,808-share RSU award
feed.summary: Greenlight Capital Re's Controller, Sherry Diaz, received an equity compensation award of 3,808 ordinary shares from restricted stock units (RSUs). These RSUs will vest evenly over three years on January 1st, contingent on her continuous service with the company. This grant increases her direct holdings to 100,379 ordinary shares, with no cash paid for the award.

feed.title: Lamb Weston earnings dip in Q3
feed.summary: Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. reported a significant dip in Q3 net income and adjusted EBITDA due to a pretax charge related to excess raw potatoes and lower-than-planned sales in its International segment. Despite the earnings decline, the company's "focus to win" strategy, implemented in July 2025, has shown progress in North America and led to facility consolidations. Lamb Weston also updated its fiscal 2026 financial targets, raising its full-year outlook for net sales and adjusted EBITDA.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma, Inc. $WSM Shares Sold by Modera Wealth Management LLC
feed.summary: Modera Wealth Management LLC reduced its stake in Williams-Sonoma (WSM) by 4.6% in Q4 2025, selling 9,004 shares, though still holding 184,739 shares worth approximately $33.0 million. Despite strong EPS of $3.04, Williams-Sonoma missed revenue expectations with sales down 4.3% year-over-year. The company increased its quarterly dividend to $0.76 per share, and institutional investors collectively own 99.29% of the company's stock, while insider selling by the CEO and CFO has also occurred recently.

feed.title: Jefferies raises Hawaiian Electric stock price target on wildfire settlement progress
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Hawaiian Electric Industries Inc. (NYSE:HE) to $13.75 from $13.25, while maintaining an Underperform rating, due to progress in its wildfire settlement with subrogation insurers withdrawing appeals. Despite this, the stock trades above both Jefferies' target and InvestingPro’s Fair Value, suggesting it may be overvalued. Key issues like rate rebasing and liability cap rulemaking are still pending, and rising oil prices could further pressure customer bills, impacting the rebasing review.

feed.title: HomeTrust Bancshares (HTB) plans first quarter 2026 earnings release
feed.summary: HomeTrust Bancshares, Inc. (HTB) has announced its intention to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings before the market opens on April 23, 2026. This information was disclosed in an 8-K filing outlining material events. The company, headquartered in Asheville, North Carolina, trades its common stock on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol HTB.

feed.title: Ecopetrol CEO Ousted by Board Amid Colombian Investigation (1)
feed.summary: The CEO of Ecopetrol SA, Ricardo Roa, has been ousted by the company's board following charges of alleged influence peddling and campaign finance violations related to Gustavo Petro's 2022 presidential campaign. Roa will take a leave of absence until June 28, during which time Juan Carlos Hurtado, the current COO, will serve as interim CEO. This change in leadership comes ahead of Colombia's next presidential election.

feed.title: Gilead Sciences to Buy Clinical-Stage Biotech Tubulis for Up to $5 Billion
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences has agreed to acquire German clinical-stage biotechnology company Tubulis for a total of up to $5 billion. The deal, comprising an initial $3.15 billion cash payment and up to $1.85 billion in contingent milestone payments, aims to strengthen Gilead's oncology pipeline. Tubulis specializes in developing next-generation antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) for cancer treatment, with lead assets targeting platinum-resistant ovarian cancer, non-small cell lung cancer, and various solid tumor types.

feed.title: RBC Adjusts Price Target on Incyte to $95 From $92, Maintains Sector Perform Rating
feed.summary: RBC has increased its price target for Incyte (NASDAQ: INCY) from $92 to $95, while keeping a "Sector Perform" rating on the stock. The adjustment reflects RBC's updated valuation for the biopharmaceutical company. This news comes amidst other recent updates for Incyte, including clinical collaborations and executive changes.

feed.title: Equity syndication and warrant dividend reshape Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ: PSKY)
feed.summary: Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has approved a charter amendment to increase Class B common shares and allow for dividend flexibility on Class B stock. This move, along with an equity syndication and the introduction of 10-year tradable warrants as a dividend to Class B shareholders, is designed to fund its proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery for $31 per share. The company believes these changes, particularly the warrant dividend, offer greater value to shareholders than its previously planned rights offering.

feed.title: Rosenblatt Securities Upgrades Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) to Buy
feed.summary: Rosenblatt Securities upgraded Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) to a "buy" rating with a $180 price target, representing a potential 42.6% upside. This upgrade follows Arista's recent earnings beat, where the company reported $0.82 EPS against an expected $0.75 and revenue of $2.49 billion, a 28.9% year-over-year increase. Despite strong institutional ownership, insiders have sold approximately 199,632 shares worth $26.9 million in the last 90 days.

feed.title: CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) Rating Lowered to Neutral at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has downgraded CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) from a "buy" to a "neutral" rating, setting a $44 target price, which implies a potential 6.1% upside. This adjustment comes despite a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating among analysts with an average target of $41.31. The transportation company also missed its quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, and insiders have recently sold a significant number of shares.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Upgrades Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ:ODFL) to Equal Weight
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has upgraded Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ:ODFL) from "underweight" to "equal weight," setting a target price of $200.00. The company's stock currently holds an average "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $192.83, following mixed analyst reports. Old Dominion Freight Line recently beat quarterly EPS estimates and narrowly topped revenue expectations, though revenue was down 5.7% year-over-year.

feed.title: F5 to Report Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: F5, Inc. announced it will report its second-quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, after the market closes. The company will host a live webcast at 4:30 p.m. ET to discuss the results with investors and analysts. Details for accessing the webcast and phone replays are provided.
